articles.title: House votes to extend Haiti protections, deepening Trump immigration fight
articles.summary: A bipartisan House vote to extend Temporary Protected Status for Haitians turned into the latest test of President Donald Trump’s immigration agenda, with Republicans split and the Senate and White House still posing steep obstacles.

articles.title: IMF says tariff shock is clouding the 2026 outlook, with inflation and growth both under strain
articles.summary: The fund’s spring forecast revision lands just as trade policy uncertainty, higher energy costs and tighter financial conditions are reshaping the global economy.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II photos sharpen the science case for a human return to the Moon
articles.summary: Fresh images from the crew’s lunar flyby are giving scientists a new look at Earth, the Moon and the deep-space environment just as NASA’s next crewed mission enters a pivotal spring stretch.

articles.title: Cannes 2026 doubles down on auteurs as the festival leans away from Hollywood
articles.summary: The 79th Cannes Film Festival unveiled a lineup heavy on international names, with Pedro Almodóvar, Paweł Pawlikowski and Ryusuke Hamaguchi among the marquee draws — and few major American studio films in sight.

articles.title: OpenAI pushes an AI-industrial policy agenda as rivals tighten safety and defense lines
articles.summary: A wave of new policy papers from OpenAI and Anthropic shows how the AI industry is trying to shape the rules that will govern its next phase — from workforce disruption and compute access to military use and frontier safety.

articles.title: Ohio’s 10-gigawatt AI campus signals a new phase in the data-center boom
articles.summary: The federal government’s plan for a massive data center and power plant in southern Ohio shows how the AI buildout is becoming an infrastructure project, not just a software story.

articles.title: Why Labubu Is Still Driving the Collectibles Economy in April 2026
articles.summary: The spiky-toothed plush figure has outgrown toy aisles, shaping resale markets, museum gift shops and a broader appetite for small, status-rich objects.

articles.title: EU’s Ukraine funding gamble turns to debt as frozen Russian assets remain locked away
articles.summary: Brussels has committed to keep Ukraine financed through 2027, but legal risk, Belgian resistance and Moscow’s retaliation show how hard it is for Europe to turn sanctions into strategy.

articles.title: Olivia Rodrigo is back with a new single as pop’s spring reset begins
articles.summary: The singer’s return arrives just as Coachella weekend two, festival chatter and a wave of 2026 releases make April one of the year’s busiest months in pop.

articles.title: IMF warns Middle East war is pushing the world toward slower growth and higher inflation
articles.summary: The fund says the conflict has become the biggest energy shock in decades, threatening vulnerable economies and raising the risk of food stress if fertilizer shipments are disrupted.
